page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It is powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors with an x86_64 architecture, providing 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores operating at 3.6 GHz. The processor features advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix operations.

The hardware configuration includes 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of 120,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration.

In benchmark testing, the i7ie.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It achieves top-tier results in Passmark CPU tests, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. The platform also shows strong capabilities in large-model LLM inference tasks.

Given its massive local NVMe SSD storage and high memory density, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads by reducing the need for external storage attachments. It is designed for large-scale databases, high-throughput transactional systems, data warehousing, and intensive ETL processing.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 192 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8559C processors running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 120,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations, including Redis SET operations. With support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX, the hardware is highly efficient for complex computational tasks. This instance is optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput transactional systems, and intensive data warehousing workloads. It offers high resource density, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring massive local storage and high-speed memory access without relying on external storage solutions.
